title: polygala vulgaris f. littoralis
intro: Polygala vulgaris f. littoralis, also known as common polygala, is a plant species that belongs to the milkwort family (Polygalaceae). It is a perennial herb that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving immune function, as well as exhibiting potential anti-cancer properties. In herbal preparations, the root and leaves of Polygala vulgaris f. littoralis are typically used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions.
intro_study: According to "Chinese journal of integrative medicine", polygala vulgaris f. littoralis, based on its similarities with Polygala sibirica L. var megalopha Fr., likely has benefits including anti-inflammatory effects by blocking the MAPK/NF-κ B pathway and anti-oxidant effects by increasing the activity of enzymes such as SOD and CAT, leading to a decrease in ROS production and an increase in anti-oxidant scavenging activity.
